title: Unit Fractions of a Whole
essential_question: How can you use a fraction to name one part of a whole that is divided into equal parts?
strategies: identify the number of equal parts (denominator)
the numerator of a unit fraction is always 1

pretest.question: A circle is divided into 3 equal parts. What unit fraction names each part?
pretest.explanation: 3 equal parts → each part is the unit fraction 1/3.

pretest.question: Which is a unit fraction?
pretest.explanation: A unit fraction has a numerator of 1. Only 1/4 has a numerator of 1.

pretest.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. 1 part is shaded. What fraction is shaded?
pretest.explanation: 1 shaded part out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.

pretest.question: In the fraction 1/8, what does the 8 tell you?
choices: The number of equal parts in the whole
pretest.explanation: The denominator (8) tells the number of equal parts the whole is divided into.

pretest.question: Which unit fraction names the largest part of a whole?
pretest.explanation: The fewer equal parts, the larger each part. 1/2 is the largest because the whole is split into only 2 parts.

learn.title: What Is a Unit Fraction?
learn.content: A unit fraction names 1 equal part of a whole. It always has 1 as the numerator. Examples: 1/2, 1/3, 1/4, 1/6, 1/8.

learn.title: Numerator and Denominator
learn.content: Every fraction has two numbers. The top number is the numerator — it tells how many parts you are counting. The bottom number is the denominator — it tells how many equal parts are in the whole. In 1/4, the numerator is 1 and the denominator is 4.

learn.title: Smaller Denominator = Larger Part
learn.content: When a whole is divided into fewer parts, each part is bigger. So 1/2 is larger than 1/4, and 1/4 is larger than 1/8. Imagine cutting a pizza into 2 slices vs. 8 slices — 2 slices are much bigger!

learn.title: Reading a Unit Fraction
learn.content: To read a unit fraction, look at the denominator. The fraction 1/3 is read 'one third' — the whole is split into 3 equal parts and you are counting 1 of them. 1/6 = 'one sixth', 1/8 = 'one eighth'.

learn.title: Writing Unit Fractions
learn.content: To write a unit fraction from a description: (1) Count the total equal parts → that is the denominator. (2) Write 1 as the numerator (always for unit fractions). Example: 'one of 6 equal parts' → 1/6.

try.question: A square is divided into 4 equal parts. What unit fraction names each part?
hints: Count the total equal parts — that is the denominator.
A unit fraction always has 1 as the numerator, so the fraction is 1/4.

feedback.correct: Right! 4 equal parts → each is the unit fraction 1/4.
feedback.incorrect: 4 equal parts → denominator is 4. Unit fraction numerator is always 1 → 1/4.

try.question: Which is NOT a unit fraction?
try.explanation: 2/3 has a numerator of 2, not 1, so it is not a unit fraction.
hints: A unit fraction always has 1 as its numerator.
Look for the fraction where the top number is NOT 1.

feedback.correct: Correct! 2/3 has numerator 2 — not a unit fraction.
feedback.incorrect: Unit fractions always have 1 on top. 2/3 has 2 on top, so it is not a unit fraction.

try.question: In the fraction 1/6, what is the numerator?
try.explanation: The numerator is the top number. In 1/6, the numerator is 1.
hints: The numerator is the top number of a fraction.
In 1/6, the top number is 1.

feedback.correct: Yes! The numerator of 1/6 is 1 (the top number).
feedback.incorrect: The numerator is the top number. In 1/6, the top number is 1.

try.question: Which unit fraction is larger: 1/4 or 1/6?
try.explanation: Fewer equal parts means each part is larger. 1/4 has 4 parts, 1/6 has 6 parts → 1/4 > 1/6.
hints: Think about pizza: would you rather cut it into 4 slices or 6 slices to get more?
Fewer parts → larger parts. 4 parts vs 6 parts → 1/4 is larger than 1/6.

feedback.correct: Right! Fewer parts = larger each part. 1/4 > 1/6.
feedback.incorrect: 4 parts vs 6 parts — fewer parts means larger parts. 1/4 is larger than 1/6.

try.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. 1 part is shaded. What unit fraction names the shaded part?
try.explanation: 1 out of 6 equal parts = unit fraction 1/6.
hints: Count the total parts: 6. That is the denominator.
1 shaded part out of 6 = 1/6.

feedback.correct: Yes! 1 shaded part out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.
feedback.incorrect: There are 6 equal parts and 1 is shaded → the unit fraction is 1/6.

practice_r1.question: A pie is divided into 8 equal slices. What unit fraction names each slice?
hints: The denominator equals the number of equal parts.
8 equal parts → each part is the unit fraction 1/8.

feedback.correct: Correct! 8 equal slices → each is 1/8.
feedback.incorrect: 8 equal slices → denominator is 8, numerator is 1 → 1/8.

practice_r1.question: A strip of paper is folded into 2 equal parts. What unit fraction is each part?
hints: 2 equal parts → denominator is 2.
1 part out of 2 = 1/2.

feedback.correct: Right! 2 equal parts → each part is 1/2.
feedback.incorrect: 2 equal parts → 1 part is 1/2.

practice_r1.question: In the fraction 1/6, what is the denominator?
practice_r1.explanation: The denominator is the bottom number. In 1/6, the denominator is 6.
hints: The denominator is the bottom number of the fraction.
In 1/6, the bottom number is 6.

feedback.correct: Yes! The denominator of 1/6 is 6 (bottom number).
feedback.incorrect: The denominator is the bottom number. In 1/6, that is 6.

practice_r1.question: A hexagon is divided into 6 equal parts. 1 part is colored. What fraction is colored?
hints: Count the total equal parts: 6. That is the denominator.
1 colored part out of 6 total = 1/6.

feedback.correct: Correct! 1 out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.
feedback.incorrect: 1 colored part out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.

practice_r1.question: Which is a unit fraction?
practice_r1.explanation: 1/3 has 1 as the numerator, so it is a unit fraction.
hints: A unit fraction always has 1 as the top number (numerator).
Find the fraction with 1 on top.

feedback.correct: Right! 1/3 has numerator 1 → it is a unit fraction.
feedback.incorrect: Unit fractions have 1 as the numerator. Only 1/3 has 1 on top.

practice_r1.question: A rectangle is divided into 3 equal parts. What unit fraction names each part?
hints: 3 equal parts → denominator is 3.
Unit fraction: 1 out of 3 = 1/3.

feedback.correct: Yes! 3 equal parts → each part is 1/3.
feedback.incorrect: 3 equal parts → 1 part = 1/3.

practice_r1.question: In the fraction 1/4, what does the 1 tell you?
practice_r1.explanation: The numerator (1) tells how many parts you are counting.
hints: The top number (numerator) counts the parts you are describing.
In 1/4, the 1 means you are counting 1 part.

feedback.correct: Correct! The numerator 1 means 1 part is being counted.
feedback.incorrect: The numerator (top number) tells how many parts are counted. In 1/4, that is 1 part.

practice_r1.question: A circle is cut into 4 equal parts. 1 part is colored blue. What unit fraction is blue?
hints: Count the total equal parts: 4. That is the denominator.
1 blue part out of 4 equal parts = 1/4.

feedback.correct: Right! 1 out of 4 equal parts = 1/4.
feedback.incorrect: 4 equal parts, 1 colored → 1/4.

practice_r1.question: In the fraction 1/3, what does the 3 mean?
choices: The whole is divided into 3 equal parts
practice_r1.explanation: The denominator 3 means the whole is divided into 3 equal parts.
hints: The bottom number (denominator) tells how many equal parts the whole is divided into.
In 1/3, the denominator is 3 → the whole has 3 equal parts.

feedback.correct: Yes! The denominator 3 means the whole is split into 3 equal parts.
feedback.incorrect: The denominator tells the total number of equal parts. In 1/3, that is 3.

practice_r1.question: Which fraction has numerator 1 AND denominator 8?
practice_r1.explanation: 1/8 has 1 as the numerator and 8 as the denominator.
hints: The numerator is the top number; the denominator is the bottom number.
Numerator 1 = top is 1. Denominator 8 = bottom is 8. That gives 1/8.

feedback.correct: Correct! 1/8 has numerator 1 and denominator 8.
feedback.incorrect: Numerator on top, denominator on bottom: 1 on top and 8 on bottom = 1/8.

practice_r2.question: A whole is divided into equal parts. Each part is 1/8. How many equal parts are there?
practice_r2.explanation: If each part is 1/8, the whole is divided into 8 equal parts.
hints: The denominator tells you how many equal parts the whole has.
In 1/8, the denominator is 8 → there are 8 equal parts.

feedback.correct: Right! 1/8 → denominator 8 → 8 equal parts.
feedback.incorrect: The denominator of 1/8 is 8, meaning there are 8 equal parts.

practice_r2.question: Which unit fraction names the smallest part of a whole?
practice_r2.explanation: More equal parts means each part is smaller. 1/8 is the smallest because the whole is split into 8 parts.
hints: More parts = smaller each part.
The largest denominator means the most parts → the smallest unit fraction.

feedback.correct: Correct! 1/8 has the most parts → each part is the smallest.
feedback.incorrect: 8 parts is more than 2, 3, or 6. More parts = smaller each part. 1/8 is smallest.

practice_r2.question: Mia says 1/6 is bigger than 1/4 because 6 > 4. Is she correct?
choices: Yes, bigger denominator means bigger fraction
No, bigger denominator means smaller parts
No, they are the same size
practice_r2.explanation: A bigger denominator means more parts, so each part is smaller. 1/6 < 1/4.
hints: Think about cutting a pizza: 6 slices vs 4 slices — which slice is bigger?
More parts (bigger denominator) → smaller each part. 1/6 < 1/4.

feedback.correct: Right! Bigger denominator = more parts = smaller each part. 1/6 < 1/4.
feedback.incorrect: More parts means each part is smaller. 1/6 has more parts than 1/4 → 1/6 is smaller.

practice_r2.question: A shape is divided into equal parts. One part is shaded, representing 1/3. How many parts does the shape have in all?
practice_r2.explanation: 1/3 means 1 out of 3 equal parts, so the shape has 3 parts in all.
hints: The denominator tells the total number of equal parts.
In 1/3, the denominator is 3 → the shape has 3 equal parts.

feedback.correct: Yes! The denominator of 1/3 is 3 → 3 equal parts total.
feedback.incorrect: 1/3 means the denominator is 3 → the shape has 3 equal parts.

practice_r2.question: Order these unit fractions from LARGEST to smallest: 1/2, 1/6, 1/4
practice_r2.explanation: Fewer parts = larger. 1/2 (2 parts) > 1/4 (4 parts) > 1/6 (6 parts).
hints: Smaller denominator = larger part.
2 < 4 < 6 for the denominators → 1/2 is largest, 1/6 is smallest.

feedback.correct: Right! 1/2 > 1/4 > 1/6 (fewer parts = larger each part).
feedback.incorrect: Compare denominators: smallest denominator = largest fraction. 2 < 4 < 6 → 1/2 > 1/4 > 1/6.

practice_r2.question: Emma says 1/8 = 1/4 because both have numerator 1. Is she correct?
choices: Yes, they are equal since both have numerator 1
Yes, unit fractions are always equal
practice_r2.explanation: The denominators are different. 1/4 is split into 4 parts and 1/8 into 8 parts. 1/4 > 1/8.
hints: Just having numerator 1 doesn't make fractions equal — the denominators matter too.
1/4 has 4 parts, 1/8 has 8 parts. Fewer parts = larger part → 1/4 > 1/8.

feedback.correct: Correct! 1/4 > 1/8 because 4 parts are bigger than 8 parts.
feedback.incorrect: Both have numerator 1 but different denominators. 4 parts > 8 parts in size → 1/4 > 1/8.

practice_r2.question: If each unit fraction is 1/6, how many of them are needed to make 1 whole?
practice_r2.explanation: 6 × (1/6) = 6/6 = 1 whole. You need 6 unit fractions of 1/6.
hints: The denominator tells you how many unit fractions make 1 whole.
1/6 has denominator 6 → you need 6 of them to make 1 whole.

feedback.incorrect: The denominator of 1/6 is 6 → you need 6 of them: 6/6 = 1 whole.

practice_r2.question: Which unit fraction is closest to 0?
practice_r2.explanation: 1/8 is the smallest unit fraction among these → it is closest to 0.
hints: A smaller unit fraction has a larger denominator and is closer to 0.
Compare: 1/2, 1/3, 1/6, 1/8. Which denominator is largest?

feedback.correct: Right! 1/8 is the smallest, so it is closest to 0.
feedback.incorrect: 1/8 has the largest denominator → smallest value → closest to 0.

practice_r2.question: A figure is divided into equal parts, each labeled 1/3. How many parts does the figure have?
practice_r2.explanation: If each part is 1/3, the denominator is 3 → 3 equal parts total.
hints: The denominator of the unit fraction tells the total number of equal parts.
1/3 → denominator 3 → 3 equal parts.

feedback.correct: Correct! 1/3 means the whole has 3 equal parts.
feedback.incorrect: The denominator of 1/3 is 3 → the figure has 3 equal parts.

practice_r2.question: Which pair lists the larger unit fraction FIRST?
practice_r2.explanation: 1/2 > 1/4 because 2 parts is fewer than 4 parts → 1/2 is larger. It is listed first.
hints: Smaller denominator = larger unit fraction.
Compare denominators: 2 < 4 → 1/2 > 1/4. Is 1/2 listed first? Yes.

feedback.correct: Yes! In choice A: 1/2 > 1/4, and 1/2 is listed first.
feedback.incorrect: 1/2 > 1/4 (fewer parts = larger). Choice A lists 1/2 first → correct order.

practice_r3.question: Sam has a ribbon. He cuts it so each piece is 1/4 of the whole ribbon. Then he cuts each piece in half. What unit fraction names each smaller piece?
practice_r3.explanation: 1/4 cut in half = 1/8. The ribbon now has 4 × 2 = 8 equal pieces.
hints: Start with 4 equal pieces (1/4 each). Then each is cut in half: 4 × 2 = 8 pieces.
8 equal pieces → each piece is 1/8 of the whole ribbon.

feedback.correct: Right! 4 pieces cut in half = 8 total → each piece is 1/8.
feedback.incorrect: 4 pieces × 2 = 8 pieces total. Each piece = 1/8 of the ribbon.

practice_r3.question: Which statement is true?
practice_r3.explanation: Fewer parts = larger pieces. 1/2 (2 parts) is greater than 1/6 (6 parts).
hints: For each statement, compare denominators: smaller denominator = larger fraction.
1/2 has denominator 2, 1/6 has denominator 6. 2 < 6 → 1/2 > 1/6. ✓

feedback.correct: Correct! 1/2 > 1/6 because 2 parts are larger than 6 parts.
feedback.incorrect: Check each statement: 1/2 has denominator 2 and 1/6 has denominator 6. 2 < 6 → 1/2 > 1/6. ✓

practice_r3.question: A pizza is cut into equal slices. Each slice is bigger than 1/6, but smaller than 1/3. What could the unit fraction for each slice be?
practice_r3.explanation: 1/4 is between 1/3 and 1/6 in size (1/3 > 1/4 > 1/6), so 1/4 works.
hints: The slice is between 1/6 and 1/3. Which fraction has a denominator between 3 and 6?
4 is between 3 and 6. So 1/4 is between 1/3 and 1/6 in size.

feedback.correct: Yes! 1/3 > 1/4 > 1/6, so 1/4 is between them.
feedback.incorrect: Find a denominator between 3 and 6: that is 4. So 1/4 fits: 1/3 > 1/4 > 1/6.

practice_r3.question: A pizza is cut so each slice = 1/4. How many slices make exactly 1/2 of the pizza?
practice_r3.explanation: 1/4 + 1/4 = 2/4 = 1/2. So 2 slices of 1/4 equal 1/2 of the pizza.
hints: How many fourths equal one half? Think: 1/2 = 2/4.
1/4 + 1/4 = 2/4 = 1/2. You need 2 slices.

feedback.correct: Right! 2 × (1/4) = 2/4 = 1/2. Two slices.
feedback.incorrect: 2 × (1/4) = 2/4 = 1/2. So 2 slices of 1/4 make half the pizza.

practice_r3.question: A whole is divided into n equal parts. As n gets larger, what happens to each unit fraction?
choices: You cannot have unit fractions for large n
practice_r3.explanation: More parts = smaller each part. As n increases, each unit fraction (1/n) gets smaller.
hints: Compare 1/2 and 1/8. Which denominator is larger? Which fraction is smaller?
1/8 < 1/2 because 8 parts means each part is smaller. More n → smaller 1/n.

feedback.correct: Correct! Larger n means more parts, so each 1/n is smaller.
feedback.incorrect: As n increases, the whole is split into more parts, so each part (1/n) gets smaller.
